title: tetragonolobus edulis
intro: Tetragonolobus edulis, also known as Horse Gram or Hara, is a perennial legume plant native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Asia, characterized by its four-angled seed pods. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is rich in nutrients and offers various health benefits, such as lowering blood sugar levels, improving digestion, and reducing the risk of heart disease. Tetragonolobus edulis can be consumed in various herbal preparations, including tea, powder, and capsules, which can be made by drying and grinding the seeds or leaves of the plant.
